text.title: Python Code Quality — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-code-quality is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Both ruff and black configured in pyproject.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Conflicting opinions; one formats, the other reformats on next run. Fix: Remove black; let Ruff format.
Root cause: Defeats the entire gate; pushes broken code. Fix: Fix the root cause; never --no-verify.
Root cause: Mutes the warning everywhere — including code you wrote. Fix: Override per-module: [[tool.mypy.overrides]] module='legacy.*'.
Root cause: Coverage drifts down with every PR; no gate. Fix: Set --cov-fail-under=80 (or project floor).

antipattern.description: No bandit / no ruff S rules in CI.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Hardcoded secrets, weak hashes ship to prod. Fix: Enable ruff S rules or run bandit in pre-commit.
